name: Battle of the Ebro Landscape Trail
name_original: La ribera del Ebro - Vuelta circular desde Maials
description: Tackle the difficult 60.3-mile road cycling route along the Ebro banks from Maials, climbing 4818 feet with diverse river views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Maials offers diverse terrain within an authentic Mediterranean mountain range, characterized by varied landscapes and elevation changes. The region features small mountain passes and winding roads, providing challenges for road cyclists. Routes often present views of the Ebro River, adding scenic value to the rides. This area provides access to significant cycling potential, including connections to the wider Ebro Natural Trail. Best road cycling routes around Maials The most popular road cycling route is Flix - La Granadella - Bellaguarda - Margalef - La Palma – circular route, a 48.5 miles (78.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 46 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied Mediterranean scenery. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Pantano de Riba-roja – circular from Riba-roja through Gandesa and Batea, a difficult 53.3 miles (85.8 km) path. This route offers extensive riding around the Riba-roja reservoir, passing through towns and providing scenic views. Local road cyclists also love the Battle of the Ebro Landscape Trail, a 60.3 miles (97.1 km) trail leading through historical landscapes and offering views of the Ebro River, often completed in about 5 hours. Road cycling around Maials is defined by Mediterranean mountain scenery, views of the Ebro River, and varied terrain with small mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Maials are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 3 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Maials's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Maials?
A: There are 19 road cycling routes around Maials, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Maials?
A: The region around Maials features an authentic Mediterranean mountain range, characterized by varied landscapes and elevation changes. You can expect winding roads, small mountain passes, and scenic views of the Ebro River, providing a diverse and engaging cycling experience.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Maials?
A: Yes, there is 1 easy road cycling route available in Maials, perfect for those new to the sport or looking for a more relaxed ride. Additionally, there are 7 moderate routes that offer a good balance of challenge and accessibility.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes around Maials?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Maials offers 11 difficult routes. Notable options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966555278/flix-la-granadella-bellaguarda-margalef-la-palma-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flix - La Granadella - Bellaguarda - Margalef - La Palma – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945275907/battle-of-the-ebro-landscape-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Battle of the Ebro Landscape Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a longer route through historical landscapes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Maials offer views of the Ebro River and pass through diverse Mediterranean scenery. You can also explore nearby attractions such as the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3020324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adar Arch&lt;/a&gt;, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2560418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco de las Pistacheras&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5340837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Islets on the Ebro River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Maials area?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966545886/pantano-de-riba-roja-circular-from-riba-roja-through-gandesa-and-batea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Riba-roja – circular from Riba-roja through Gandesa and Batea&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive riding around the Riba-roja reservoir, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14893427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaça Major Flix – Riba-roja Reservoir loop from Flix&lt;/a&gt; is another great option.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Maials?
A: The road cycling routes in Maials are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied Mediterranean scenery, the challenging mountain passes, and the beautiful views of the Ebro River that define the region's rides.

Q: Can I find routes that connect to the Ebro Natural Trail?
A: Yes, the Maials area provides access to significant cycling potential, including connections to the wider Ebro Natural Trail. While the trail has unpaved sections, there are asphalted parts that wind through riverside woodland and offer views of the Ebro.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes from Maials?
A: For longer rides, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20175451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Seu Vella de Lleida – Old Village of Mequinenza loop from Maials&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 125 km, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945275907/battle-of-the-ebro-landscape-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Battle of the Ebro Landscape Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 97 km route through historical landscapes.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on road cycling routes in Maials?
A: The road cycling routes in Maials feature varied elevation changes due to the Mediterranean mountain terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966555278/flix-la-granadella-bellaguarda-margalef-la-palma-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flix - La Granadella - Bellaguarda - Margalef - La Palma – circular route&lt;/a&gt; has over 1000 meters of elevation gain, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966545886/pantano-de-riba-roja-circular-from-riba-roja-through-gandesa-and-batea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Riba-roja – circular from Riba-roja through Gandesa and Batea&lt;/a&gt; includes over 1200 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through towns or villages where I can stop?
A: Many routes in the Maials area pass through or near charming towns and villages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966545886/pantano-de-riba-roja-circular-from-riba-roja-through-gandesa-and-batea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Riba-roja – circular from Riba-roja through Gandesa and Batea&lt;/a&gt; takes you through Gandesa and Batea, offering opportunities for breaks and refreshments.
tips: Square of medieval origin. Around 1360, King Pedro III granted the town the privilege of holding a weekly market every Tuesday, which was held in this same square. A good place to rest for a while.
Riba-roja is located on the right bank of the Ebro, on a fluvial terrace. The streets of the old town are narrow and you can also see the old walled town.
Good views of the town of La Granadella. The villa is located on a hill at an altitude of 528 meters, in the district of Les Garrigues, bordering the districts of Segrià and Ribera d'Ebre.

faqs.question: Is The banks of the Ebro - Circular route from Maials suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this is classified as a "difficult" racebike route, requiring a high level of fitness. Cyclists should be prepared for continuous climbing and some segments that may be unpaved and challenging to ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route predominantly features asphalt and paved roads, making up the majority of its length. However, there are also some unpaved sections, so be prepared for varied surfaces. You'll experience light and constant ascents and descents throughout.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 97 km long with significant elevation gain, and it typically takes around 5 hours to complete. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What scenic viewpoints or landmarks can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter diverse scenery along the Ebro River, including canyons, meanders, and historical villages. Specific highlights include Plaça Major Flix, the town of Riba-roja d'Ebre, and a notable View of La Granadella. The route also offers views of the Ribarroja Reservoir and traditional riverside orchards.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with milder temperatures. While summer can be warm, the presence of rest areas and fresh water points along the route can make it manageable. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park when starting this route in Maials?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in Maials, you can look for public parking options within the town. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak cycling seasons, to secure a convenient spot.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebiking and covers long distances on roads and some unpaved sections. While dogs are generally allowed in public areas, bringing a dog on this specific type of challenging cycling route is not recommended for their safety or comfort.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and trails along the Ebro banks.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like rest areas or water points along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several villages and areas where you can find rest areas and fresh water points. These can be particularly useful during warmer months for refilling water bottles.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths, including the E38, Paissatges de la Batalla de l'Ebre, and E37.

faqs.question: What kind of historical or cultural sights can I see along the Ebro banks?
faqs.answer: The Ebro River basin is rich in history. You can expect to see historical villages, Romanesque buildings, and structures like bridges, dams, canals, and mills that showcase centuries of human interaction with the river. The river itself has been a significant 'bi-directional highway' for people and ideas.
